result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  collect                   Collects a diagnostic trace from a currently running process
  ps                        Lists the dotnet processes that gcdumps can be collected from.
  report <gcdump_filename>  Generate report into stdout from a previously generated gcdump or from a running process.
  convert <input>           Converts nettrace file into .gcdump file handled by analysis tools. Can only convert from the nettrace format.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  collect                   Collects a diagnostic trace from a currently running process
  ps                        Lists the dotnet processes that gcdumps can be collected from.
  report <gcdump_filename>  Generate report into stdout from a previously generated gcdump or from a running process.
  convert <input>           Converts nettrace file into .gcdump file handled by analysis tools. Can only convert from the nettrace format.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Collects a diagnostic trace from a currently running process

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump collect [options]

Options:
  -p, --process-id            The process id to collect the gcdump from.
  -o, --output                The path where collected gcdumps should be written. Defaults to '.\YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_<pid>.gcdump' where YYYYMMDD is Year/Month/Day and HHMMSS is Hour/Minute/Second. Otherwise, it is the full path and file name of the dump.
  -v, --verbose               Output the log while collecting the gcdump.
  -t, --timeout               Give up on collecting the gcdump if it takes longer than this many seconds. The default value is 30s. [default: 30]
  -n, --name                  The name of the process to collect the gcdump from.
  --diagnostic-port, --dport  The path to a diagnostic port to collect the dump from.
  --dsrouter                  The dsrouter command to use for collecting the gcdump. If specified, the --process-id, --name, or --diagnostic-port options cannot be used.
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Collects a diagnostic trace from a currently running process

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump collect [options]

Options:
  -p, --process-id            The process id to collect the gcdump from.
  -o, --output                The path where collected gcdumps should be written. Defaults to '.\YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_<pid>.gcdump' where YYYYMMDD is Year/Month/Day and HHMMSS is Hour/Minute/Second. Otherwise, it is the full path and file name of the dump.
  -v, --verbose               Output the log while collecting the gcdump.
  -t, --timeout               Give up on collecting the gcdump if it takes longer than this many seconds. The default value is 30s. [default: 30]
  -n, --name                  The name of the process to collect the gcdump from.
  --diagnostic-port, --dport  The path to a diagnostic port to collect the dump from.
  --dsrouter                  The dsrouter command to use for collecting the gcdump. If specified, the --process-id, --name, or --diagnostic-port options cannot be used.
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Converts nettrace file into .gcdump file handled by analysis tools. Can only convert from the nettrace format.

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump convert [<input>] [options]

Arguments:
  <input>  Input trace file to be converted.

Options:
  -o, --output    The path where converted gcdump should be written. Defaults to '<input>.gcdump'
  -v, --verbose   Output the log while converting the gcdump.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Converts nettrace file into .gcdump file handled by analysis tools. Can only convert from the nettrace format.

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump convert [<input>] [options]

Arguments:
  <input>  Input trace file to be converted.

Options:
  -o, --output    The path where converted gcdump should be written. Defaults to '<input>.gcdump'
  -v, --verbose   Output the log while converting the gcdump.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Lists the dotnet processes that gcdumps can be collected from.

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump ps [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate report into stdout from a previously generated gcdump or from a running process.

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump report [<gcdump_filename>] [options]

Arguments:
  <gcdump_filename>  The file to read gcdump from.

Options:
  -p, --process-id              The process id to collect the gcdump from.
  -t, --report-type <HeapStat>  The type of report to generate. Available options: heapstat (default) [default: HeapStat]
  --diagnostic-port, --dport    The path to a diagnostic port to collect the dump from.
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-gcdump report [<gcdump_filename>] [options]

Arguments:
  <gcdump_filename>  The file to read gcdump from.

Options:
  -p, --process-id              The process id to collect the gcdump from.
  -t, --report-type <HeapStat>  The type of report to generate. Available options: heapstat (default) [default: HeapStat]
  --diagnostic-port, --dport    The path to a diagnostic port to collect the dump from.
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information
